Andhra Pradesh.name: Kondapalli Toys (GI)
Andhra Pradesh.materials: Tella Poniki wood (softwood), tamarind seed paste, enamel paints
Andhra Pradesh.description: Lightweight, hand-carved wooden toys depicting rural life, mythological figures, and animals.

Andhra Pradesh.name: Srikalahasti Kalamkari (GI)
Andhra Pradesh.materials: Cotton fabric, bamboo pen (kalam), natural plant dyes, milk
Andhra Pradesh.description: A freehand drawing textile art style where religious themes and epics are painted directly onto fabric.

Andhra Pradesh.name: Machilipatnam Kalamkari (GI)
Andhra Pradesh.materials: Cotton fabric, hand-carved teakwood blocks, vegetable dyes
Andhra Pradesh.description: Block-printed textile art displaying complex Persian-influenced floral patterns, trees of life, and borders.

Andhra Pradesh.name: Etikoppaka Toys (GI)
Andhra Pradesh.materials: Ankudu wood (softwood), natural lacquer, organic seed and root dyes
Andhra Pradesh.description: Turned-wood toys and artifacts coated with brilliant, non-toxic colored lacquer resins.

Andhra Pradesh.name: Dharmavaram Silk Sarees (GI)
Andhra Pradesh.materials: Pure mulberry silk, gold and silver zari threads
Andhra Pradesh.description: Heavy wedding sarees recognized by broad solid borders and shaded pallus created by dual-tone weaving.

Telangana.name: Cheriyal Scroll Painting (GI)
Telangana.materials: Khadi cotton cloth, tamarind seed paste, white mud, natural dyes
Telangana.description: A stylized narrative art style painted on long fabric scrolls to illustrate local folklore and epics.

Telangana.name: Pembarthi Metal Craft (GI)
Telangana.description: Exquisite sheet metal embossing work historically used to ornament temple chariots, doors, and individual shields.

Telangana.name: Bidriware (GI)
Telangana.materials: Zinc and Copper alloy, pure silver wire, Bidar fort soil
Telangana.description: A striking metal handicraft where fine silver wires are inlaid into a chemically blackened zinc-copper base.

Telangana.name: Pochampally Ikat (GI)
Telangana.materials: Mulberry silk or cotton yarn, natural and synthetic dyes
Telangana.description: A precision tie-dye weaving method (Chakka Jamdani) creating bold geometric patterns across the fabric warp and weft.

Telangana.name: Nirmal Toys and Paintings (GI)
Telangana.materials: Burgu wood (softwood), luppa (herbal paste), oil colors
Telangana.description: Traditional wooden toys and furniture painted with fine gold-hued lacquer finishes detailing classical themes.

Karnataka.name: Channapatna Toys (GI)
Karnataka.materials: Ivory wood (Aale mara), vegetable dyes, natural lacquer
Karnataka.description: Perfectly rounded wooden toys polished on high-speed lathes using non-toxic lacquer extracted from cacti seeds.

Karnataka.name: Mysore Sandalwood Carving
Karnataka.description: Highly intricate relief carving on aromatic sandalwood, producing delicate figurines and structural caskets.

Karnataka.name: Bidriware (Bidar) (GI)
Karnataka.materials: Zinc-copper base alloy, silver sheets, ammonium chloride soil
Karnataka.description: The geographical source craft of silver inlay work blackened using centuries-old subterranean soil recipes.

Karnataka.name: Kinhal Toys (GI)
Karnataka.materials: Polki wood, liquid gold stencil foil, kitta (sawdust paste)
Karnataka.description: Ornate wooden models of deities and royal birds assembled with specialized paste joints and gold plating.

Karnataka.name: Navalgund Durries (GI)
Karnataka.materials: Coarse cotton yarn, floor upright looms
Karnataka.description: Rare, tightly woven geometric floor rugs featuring complex chessboards and bird designs made by hand without mechanical combs.

Kerala.name: Aranmula Kannadi (GI)
Kerala.materials: Copper and Tin alloy glass-less mirror, brass framing
Kerala.description: A highly secretive, polished metallurgical alloy mirror giving zero secondary distortion, unlike silvered glass.

Kerala.name: Screw Pine Craft (GI)
Kerala.materials: Leaves of the Screw Pine plant (Pandanus), natural dyes
Kerala.description: Traditional weaving of split, cured screw pine leaves into fine straw mats, bags, and household containers.

Kerala.name: Maddalam and Chenda Making
Kerala.materials: Jackwood trunk core, buffalo skin, cowhide, paste strings
Kerala.description: Precision structural woodwork and leather curing to forge heavy classical percussion instruments used in Kathakali.

Kerala.name: Balaramapuram Sarees & Fine Cotton (GI)
Kerala.materials: Superfine grey cotton cotton yarn, pure gold zari threads
Kerala.description: Traditional handloom cream-colored fabrics woven with identical ornamental motifs appearing on both faces.

Tamil Nadu.name: Tanjore Painting (GI)
Tamil Nadu.materials: Plywood base, canvas, semi-precious stones, pure gold foil, lime paste
Tamil Nadu.description: Iconic religious relief paintings featuring flat surfaces layered with glittering 22k gold leaf accents.

Tamil Nadu.name: Swamimalai Bronze Icons (GI)
Tamil Nadu.materials: Copper, Zinc, Lead, Tin alloy (Panchaloha), beeswax, fine clay
Tamil Nadu.description: Lost-wax cast bronze sculptures forged following strict iconometric rules defined in ancient Shilpa Shastras.

Tamil Nadu.name: Pattamadai Mats (GI)
Tamil Nadu.materials: Korai grass reeds, cotton silk threads
Tamil Nadu.description: Fine mats woven so split-thin from local river grass that they resemble silk fabric and can be folded completely.

Tamil Nadu.name: Nachiarkoil Brass Lamp (GI)
Tamil Nadu.description: Hand-cast tiered brass lamps (Kuthuvilakku) accented with complex hollow ornamental spires and bird toppers.

Tamil Nadu.name: Toda Embroidery (GI)
Tamil Nadu.materials: Coarse white cotton canvas, thick red and black wool thread
Tamil Nadu.description: Distinctive geometric embroidery done entirely by memory from the reverse side, mimicking woven textures.

Andaman & Nicobar.name: Andaman Shell Craft
Andaman & Nicobar.materials: Turbo, Trochus, and Mother-of-Pearl sea shells
Andaman & Nicobar.description: Hand-ground and polished marine shells fashioned into functional jewelry, table lamps, and fine inlay tokens.

Andaman & Nicobar.name: Nicobarese Cane Basketry
Andaman & Nicobar.materials: Local wild forest cane splits, bamboo ribbons
Andaman & Nicobar.description: Extremely sturdy, deep nested storage baskets hand-woven natively by the Nicobarese tribal groups.

Lakshadweep.name: Lakshadweep Coir Crafts
Lakshadweep.materials: Coconut husk fibers, sea water retting pits
Lakshadweep.description: Hand-twisted golden coir mats, ropes, and dense corridor runners utilizing specialized marine-cured fibers.

Lakshadweep.name: Coral Shell Carving
Lakshadweep.description: Artisanal hand-etching on naturally cast-away beach shells and hard lime matrices to create local souvenirs.

Puducherry.name: Terracotta of Vilianur (GI)
Puducherry.materials: Local pond clay, river silt, natural mineral washes
Puducherry.description: Ornate terracotta pottery and large garden statues displaying stylized figures of structural deities.

Puducherry.name: Kora Grass Weaving
Puducherry.description: Locally woven table-mats and floor coverings colored using eco-friendly natural plant dyes.

Chandigarh.name: Le Corbusier Pierre Jeanneret Furniture
Chandigarh.materials: Solid teakwood, hand-woven Indian cane splits
Chandigarh.description: Mid-century modernist furniture pieces (like V-leg chairs) handmade by local Punjabi carpenters following historic architectural blueprints.

Chandigarh.name: Phulkari Adaptation
Chandigarh.description: Hand-embroidered winter fabrics practiced locally in surrounding rural enclaves following traditional migratory patterns.

Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu.name: Warli Painting Adaptation
Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu.materials: Rice paste mixture, red ochre canvas mud boards
Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu.description: Tribal wall artwork sharing cultural roots with surrounding border regions, detailing community lifestyle patterns.

Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu.name: Tortoiseshell and Ivory Style Carving
Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu.materials: Polishing resins, synthetic replica horns, dense wood cores
Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu.description: Historic decorative carving style native to Diu, now adapted using eco-friendly horn or synthetic resin variants.

Bihar.name: Madhubani Painting (GI)
Bihar.materials: Handmade paper, canvas, natural pigments, twigs, matchsticks
Bihar.description: Also known as Mithila art, this traditional painting style uses line drawings filled with bright, natural colors to depict mythology and nature.

Bihar.name: Sujini Embroidery (GI)
Bihar.materials: Layered old cotton fabric, colorful cotton threads
Bihar.description: A traditional quilt embroidery technique that uses simple running stitches to depict narrative scenes of social issues and rural life.

Bihar.name: Sikki Grass Craft (GI)
Bihar.materials: Sikki grass, golden wild grass, organic dyes
Bihar.description: An ancient craft where a specific wild golden grass is dried, dyed, and woven into sturdy, artistic containers, toys, and mats.

Jharkhand.name: Sohrai Art (GI)
Jharkhand.materials: Mud walls, natural earth soils (ochre, black, white mud), chewing sticks
Jharkhand.description: A ritualistic wall painting art practiced by tribal women during harvest festivals, featuring bold lines, zoomorphic forms, and plant motifs.

Jharkhand.name: Paitkar Painting
Jharkhand.materials: Scrolls of paper or leaves, natural tree-leaf and stone pigments
Jharkhand.description: One of the oldest tribal scroll-painting traditions in India, used by cultural storytellers to illustrate life after death and tribal legends.

West Bengal.name: Bankura Terracotta (GI)
West Bengal.description: Renowned for its distinctive, stylized terracotta horses and elephants characterized by elongated ears and hollow structures.

West Bengal.name: Dokra Metal Craft (GI)
West Bengal.materials: Brass scrap, beeswax, fine clay, resin
West Bengal.description: An ancient non-ferrous lost-wax casting technique used to fashion intricate primitive figurines and rustic home decor.

West Bengal.name: Madurkathi Mat Weaving (GI)
West Bengal.description: Traditional organic floor mats woven meticulously from rhizome reeds, known for their coolness and resilience to tropical climates.

Odisha.name: Pattachitra Painting (GI)
Odisha.materials: Cloth canvas, tamarind seed paste, chalk powder, mineral colors
Odisha.description: A highly detailed, structured scroll painting tradition executing mythological narratives with fine lines and bold borders.

Odisha.name: Pipili Applique Work (GI)
Odisha.materials: Cotton cloth sheets, mirrors, embroidery threads
Odisha.description: Artistic cutting and stitching of colorful fabric shapes onto a base cloth, heavily used for umbrellas and canopies during the Rath Yatra.

Odisha.name: Cuttack Silver Filigree - Tarakasi (GI)
Odisha.description: An extremely delicate metal craft where fine silver wire is beaten and curled into complex lace-like jewelry and models.

Sikkim.name: Choktsee Tables
Sikkim.materials: Tooni wood (Red Cedar), oil paints, gold polish
Sikkim.description: Exquisitely hand-carved, foldable wooden tables boasting dense Tibetan Buddhist symbolic motifs and relief patterns.

Sikkim.name: Thangka Painting
Sikkim.materials: Cotton canvas, gold dust, mineral colors, yak-skin glue
Sikkim.description: Detailed sacred scroll paintings illustrating Buddhist deities, mandalas, and philosophy, demanding strict geometric proportions.

Assam.name: Muga Silk Weaving (GI)
Assam.description: A prestigious, naturally shimmering golden silk handloom textile that increases in luster with every hand wash.

Assam.name: Majuli Mask Making
Assam.materials: Bamboo frames, clay, cow dung, cloth, natural colors
Assam.description: Traditional dramatic masks hand-molded over bamboo skeletons in the Vaishnavite monasteries (Satras) of Majuli island.

Assam.name: Assam Bamboo and Cane Craft
Assam.materials: Indigenous bamboo species (Jati, Bhaluka), rattan cane
Assam.description: A widespread utility craft delivering fine woven storage baskets, structural panels, furniture, and the iconic Jaapi sunshade hat.

Arunachal Pradesh.name: Monpa Handmade Paper (GI)
Arunachal Pradesh.materials: Bark of the Shugu Sheng tree (Daphne plant)
Arunachal Pradesh.description: A centuries-old tree-free papermaking process resulting in a fibrous, moth-resistant texture favored for Buddhist scriptures.

Arunachal Pradesh.name: Apatani Textile Weaving (GI)
Arunachal Pradesh.description: Woven fabrics featuring geometric zig-zag patterns and bold, dark stripes, central to tribal identity and ceremonies.

Meghalaya.name: Milliang Mat (Tlieng Weaving)
Meghalaya.description: Highly complex, gloss-finished woven mats that are exceptionally durable and waterproof, native to the Khasi hills.

Meghalaya.name: Ryndia Silk Weaving
Meghalaya.description: An eco-friendly, warm silk fabric harvested without killing the silkworms, hand-woven on traditional floor looms.

Nagaland.name: Naga Shawls (GI)
Nagaland.materials: Raw cotton, wool, backstrap loom setup
Nagaland.description: Deeply symbolic textiles woven with specific black, red, and blue stripes that indicate the social status and achievements of the wearer.

Nagaland.name: Naga Bamboo Mug Craft
Nagaland.materials: Green bamboo nodes, cane strips, wood sap colors
Nagaland.description: Hand-carved drinking mugs frequently decorated with etched geometric carvings or painted motifs of warrior traditions.

Manipur.name: Longpi Black Pottery
Manipur.materials: Black serpentinite stone, local weathered clay
Manipur.description: A unique, wheel-less pottery technique where items are hand-formed from ground rock, fired, and polished shiny with local leaves.

Manipur.name: Shaphee Lanphee (GI)
Manipur.materials: Cotton base cloth, red/black/yellow wool yarns
Manipur.description: A traditional embroidered fabric featuring stylized stars, horses, and elephants, historically gifted to brave warriors by the king.

Mizoram.name: Puan Handloom Weaving (GI)
Mizoram.description: Traditional Mizo textiles possessing intricate horizontal diamond patterns and warp-stripes essential for weddings and dances.

Mizoram.name: Hnatial Bamboo Basketry
Mizoram.materials: Local wild bamboo fibers, cane splits
Mizoram.description: Finely split, smoke-dried basketry presenting highly intricate structural diagonal weaves designed for mountain farming.

Tripura.name: Tripura Bamboo Screen and Mat Craft
Tripura.description: Artistic screens, partitions, and fine floor mats woven so delicately that the panels look almost canvas-soft.

Tripura.name: Rignai Weaving
Tripura.description: An indigenous lower-garment textile woven with localized 'Chunya' patterns distinct to individual tribal clans.

Rajasthan.name: Jaipur Blue Pottery (GI)
Rajasthan.materials: Quartz powder, raw glass, multani mitti, gum, water
Rajasthan.description: An exquisite clay-free traditional pottery style painted with cobalt blue dyes and unique floral or animal motifs.

Rajasthan.name: Kathputli of Rajasthan (GI)
Rajasthan.materials: Mango wood (Ardu wood), cotton threads, colorful fabrics
Rajasthan.description: Traditional wooden string puppets handcrafted by the Bhatt community, highly celebrated in local folk theater and storytelling.

Rajasthan.name: Sanganeri Hand Block Printing (GI)
Rajasthan.materials: Cambric cotton, carved teakwood blocks, natural dyes
Rajasthan.description: Delicate fabric printing known for fine, detailed floral motifs (butas) stamped neatly on an off-white background.

Rajasthan.name: Thewa Artwork (GI)
Rajasthan.materials: 23-karat pure gold foil, multi-colored treated glass (Belgian glass)
Rajasthan.description: An ultra-fine jewelry craft involving the fusion of highly intricate, hand-carved patterned gold foil sheets onto molten colored glass.

Rajasthan.name: Molela Terracotta Clay Work (GI)
Rajasthan.materials: Local clay, donkey dung, natural mineral glazes
Rajasthan.description: Hollow, flat terracotta relief plaques depicting folk deities and village scenes, crafted by hand along the Banas riverbed.

Rajasthan.name: Kota Doria Weaving (GI)
Rajasthan.materials: Fine Cotton, Mulberry Silk, real Zari threads
Rajasthan.description: Lightweight, translucent handloom fabric characterized by distinct woven square checkers called 'khats'.

Gujarat.name: Patan Patola (GI)
Gujarat.materials: Pure mulberry silk, natural organic vegetable dyes
Gujarat.description: The absolute pinnacle of double-ikat weaving, where both warp and weft threads are tie-dyed identically before loom assembly.

Gujarat.name: Kutch Embroidery (GI)
Gujarat.materials: Cotton or silk fabric, vibrant silk threads, tiny mirrors (abhla)
Gujarat.description: Intricate tribal needlework variations (Ahir, Rabari, Mutwa) incorporating mirror work into bright geometric patterns.

Gujarat.name: Rogan Painting (GI)
Gujarat.materials: Castor oil residue, natural mineral pigments, metal stylus
Gujarat.description: A rare art where thick, gel-like boiled castor paste is stretched into fine threads via a stylus to paint freehand on fabric.

Gujarat.name: Sankheda Furniture (GI)
Gujarat.materials: Teakwood, lacquer resin, metallic gold/maroon pigments
Gujarat.description: Vibrant wooden furniture turned on hand-lathes, coated with natural lacquer, and painted with fine ornamental patterns.

Gujarat.name: Pithora Painting (GI)
Gujarat.materials: Wall plaster, milk, ghee, local pigments
Gujarat.description: A highly sacred ritualistic wall painting style of the Rathwa and Bhil tribes, featuring dynamic depictions of horses and nature.

Maharashtra.name: Paithani Sarees & Fabrics (GI)
Maharashtra.materials: Fine mulberry silk, pure silver and gold zari wires
Maharashtra.description: Luxury handloom sarees defined by an oblique square-design border and an opulent pallu featuring tapestry-weave peacocks.

Maharashtra.name: Warli Painting (GI)
Maharashtra.materials: Mud-plaster base, rice paste, water, bamboo sticks
Maharashtra.description: A minimalist indigenous tribal art form using geometric circles, triangles, and squares to portray community dances and harvests.

Maharashtra.name: Kolhapuri Chappal (GI)
Maharashtra.materials: Bag-tanned buffalo and goat leather, seed pods for coloration
Maharashtra.description: Sturdy, completely hand-stitched leather slip-ons embellished with braided leather cords and a signature side flap.

Goa.name: Goan Azulejos (GI)
Goa.description: Hand-painted, blue-and-white vitrified ceramic tiles reflecting a distinct Portuguese-influenced colonial artistic heritage.

Goa.name: Kunbi Saree
Goa.description: Traditional checks woven natively by the tribal Kunbi community, highly sturdy and built for historic agricultural work.

Madhya Pradesh.name: Chanderi Fabric (GI)
Madhya Pradesh.materials: Degummed silk warp, fine cotton weft, gold zari
Madhya Pradesh.description: An extraordinarily lightweight, sheer, and glossy handloom fabric woven with intricate boutique booties.

Madhya Pradesh.name: Maheshwari Handloom (GI)
Madhya Pradesh.description: Elegant textiles known for their minimalist reversible geometric borders inspired by the stone carvings of Maheshwar Fort.

Madhya Pradesh.name: Bagh Prints (GI)
Madhya Pradesh.materials: Cotton or silk fabric, river water, alizarin, iron rust blocks
Madhya Pradesh.description: A sophisticated block-printing technique relying on extensive river-washing steps and natural red/black vegetable pigments.

Madhya Pradesh.name: Gond Painting (GI)
Madhya Pradesh.materials: Handmade canvas, acrylics or natural sap, fine lining pens
Madhya Pradesh.description: A vivid tribal art form using dense patterns of dots, lines, and dashes to delineate folklore, animals, and local nature systems.

Chhattisgarh.name: Bastar Dhokra Bell Metal (GI)
Chhattisgarh.materials: Brass/Bronze scrap, beeswax, riverbed sand, clay
Chhattisgarh.description: An ancient, non-ferrous lost-wax casting craft used by tribal groups to forge hollow, textured tribal figurines.

Chhattisgarh.name: Bastar Iron Craft (GI)
Chhattisgarh.materials: Wrought iron, charcoal kilns, anvil hammers
Chhattisgarh.description: Minimalist, elongated iron artifacts and lamps forged from scrap iron by the historic Agaria blacksmith community.

Jammu & Kashmir.name: Pashmina Shawl (GI)
Jammu & Kashmir.description: Exceptionally soft, hand-spun and woven luxury shawls known for their warmth and lightness.

Jammu & Kashmir.name: Kani Shawl (GI)
Jammu & Kashmir.description: Intricately woven shawls where patterns are created loom-stage using color-coded wooden sticks.

Jammu & Kashmir.name: Kashmir Papier-Mâché (GI)
Jammu & Kashmir.materials: Paper pulp, rice glue, natural colors, gold leaf
Jammu & Kashmir.description: Delicate decorative objects featuring intricate, vibrant Persian-influenced floral painting.

Jammu & Kashmir.name: Walnut Wood Carving (GI)
Jammu & Kashmir.description: Deep undercut and relief carving used on furniture and decorative items, featuring floral motifs.

Jammu & Kashmir.name: Khatamband (GI)
Jammu & Kashmir.description: An architectural woodwork craft where geometric wooden pieces are fitted together without nails for ceilings.

Jammu & Kashmir.name: Basohli Painting (GI)
Jammu & Kashmir.description: Distinctive Pahari paintings characterized by bold colors, facial profiles, and depictions of deities.

Jammu & Kashmir.name: Willow Wicker (Shaakhsaazi)
Jammu & Kashmir.description: Traditional basketry and furniture weaving providing essential livelihood in the Kashmir valley.

Jammu & Kashmir.name: Sozni Embroidery (GI)
Jammu & Kashmir.materials: Pashmina or wool base, silk/cotton thread
Jammu & Kashmir.description: Extremely fine needlework using satin stitch, frequently employed to decorate Pashmina shawls.

Himachal Pradesh.name: Kullu Shawl (GI)
Himachal Pradesh.materials: Merino wool, Angora, local sheep wool
Himachal Pradesh.description: Handwoven shawls defined by their bright, geometric, multi-colored borders against a plain background.

Himachal Pradesh.name: Kangra Painting (GI)
Himachal Pradesh.materials: Handmade paper, mineral and vegetable colors
Himachal Pradesh.description: Lyrical and naturalistic Pahari miniature paintings heavily inspired by the Radha-Krishna lore.

Himachal Pradesh.name: Chamba Rumal (GI)
Himachal Pradesh.description: Double-sided pictorial embroidery historically used to cover offerings to deities or as royal gifts.

Himachal Pradesh.name: Kinnauri Shawl (GI)
Himachal Pradesh.description: Heavy winter textiles renowned for complex, central-Asian-influenced geometric motifs.

Himachal Pradesh.name: Lahaul Socks & Gloves (GI)
Himachal Pradesh.description: Intricately knitted winter wear made in the Lahaul-Spiti region using ancient 4-needle techniques.

Himachal Pradesh.name: Chamba Chappal (GI)
Himachal Pradesh.description: Traditional handcrafted leather footwear embellished with vivid silk thread embroidery.

Punjab.name: Phulkari (GI)
Punjab.description: Bright, floral-patterned embroidery done from the reverse side of the fabric.

Punjab.name: Thathera Metal Craft
Punjab.description: Handcrafted brass and copper utensils created by hammering metal into shape (UNESCO recognized).

Punjab.name: Punjabi Jutti
Punjab.description: Flat, embroidered leather slip-on footwear featuring no left-right distinction until broken in.

Punjab.name: Panja Durries
Punjab.description: Hand-loomed thick floor rugs made using a metallic claw (Panja) to beat the weft tightly.

Uttarakhand.name: Aipan Art (GI)
Uttarakhand.description: Traditional ritualistic floor and wall painting of the Kumaon region featuring geometric deity motifs.

Uttarakhand.name: Ringal Craft (GI)
Uttarakhand.description: Highly durable, woven bamboo items including mats, baskets, and agricultural tools made by hill artisans.

Uttarakhand.name: Tamta Copperware (GI)
Uttarakhand.description: Hand-beaten copper vessels and religious artifacts historically crafted by the Tamta community of Almora.

Uttarakhand.name: Thulma Blankets (GI)
Uttarakhand.description: Exceptionally thick, hand-woven, and brushed fuzzy blankets native to high-altitude regions.

Uttarakhand.name: Bhotiya Dann (GI)
Uttarakhand.description: Traditional hand-knotted woolen carpets created by the Bhotiya tribe, characterized by thick pile.

Uttarakhand.name: Himalayan Nettle Craft
Uttarakhand.description: Eco-friendly textiles, bags, and ropes woven from wild stinging nettle for exceptional strength.

Uttarakhand.name: Likhai Wood Carving (GI)
Uttarakhand.description: Ornate wood carving traditionally used on the window frames and doorways of Kumaoni architecture.

Haryana.name: Sarkanda Craft
Haryana.description: Golden-colored woven furniture (like Pidhas/stools) and baskets created from wild river grass.

Haryana.name: Surahi Pottery
Haryana.description: Thin-necked, unglazed earthen water pitchers from Jhajjar, known for rapid water-cooling properties.

Haryana.name: Tilla Jutti (Rewari)
Haryana.description: Embellished slip-on leather footwear known for heavy gold/silver wire embroidery on the toe.

Delhi.name: Blue Pottery
Delhi.description: Features a deeper indigo hue with a robust design language, distinct from Jaipur's variant.

Delhi.name: Zardozi Embroidery
Delhi.description: Heavy, opulent 3D metallic embroidery originally patronized by the Mughals, centered in Old Delhi.

Delhi.name: Meenakari
Delhi.description: The intricate art of decorating metal surfaces with vibrant, fused glass enamel.

Uttar Pradesh.name: Varanasi Brocade & Sarees (GI)
Uttar Pradesh.description: Masterpiece handloom sarees woven with highly detailed metallic floral/paisley brocades.

Uttar Pradesh.name: Lucknow Chikan Craft (GI)
Uttar Pradesh.description: A delicate, shadow-work embroidery technique consisting of 36 distinct stitching styles.

Uttar Pradesh.name: Moradabad Metal Craft (GI)
Uttar Pradesh.description: Highly polished and engraved brassware, ranging from decorative trays to utilitarian pots.

Uttar Pradesh.name: Saharanpur Wood Craft (GI)
Uttar Pradesh.description: Intricately carved wooden screens, furniture, and boxes known for brass inlay (Tarkashi).

Uttar Pradesh.name: Bhadohi Handmade Carpet (GI)
Uttar Pradesh.description: The largest hand-knotted carpet weaving belt in South Asia, producing dense, Persian-style rugs.

Uttar Pradesh.name: Nizamabad Black Pottery (GI)
Uttar Pradesh.description: Striking black baked clay pottery with silver-colored patterns rubbed into incised grooves.

Uttar Pradesh.name: Firozabad Glass Craft (GI)
Uttar Pradesh.description: India's epicenter for glassmaking, renowned for handcrafted glass bangles and decorative glassware.

Uttar Pradesh.name: Khurja Pottery (GI)
Uttar Pradesh.description: Colorful, hand-painted ceramic stoneware and tableware utilizing a double-firing process.

Uttar Pradesh.name: Gorakhpur Terracotta (GI)
Uttar Pradesh.description: Intricately ornamented, unglazed terracotta animal figurines, most notably towering clay horses.

Uttar Pradesh.name: Agra Leather Footwear (GI)
Uttar Pradesh.description: A massive hub of skilled leather artisans handcrafting formal shoes, juttis, and leather accessories.

Uttar Pradesh.name: Kalpi Handmade Paper (GI)
Uttar Pradesh.description: Traditional, chemical-free, wood-free paper making dating back centuries.

Uttar Pradesh.name: Banaras Metal Repousse (GI)
Uttar Pradesh.description: 3D embossed metalwork achieved by hammering from the reverse side, used in religious objects.

Uttar Pradesh.name: Farrukhabad Prints (GI)
Uttar Pradesh.description: Traditional hand-block printing utilizing classical 'Tree of Life' motifs and dense geometrical patterns.

Uttar Pradesh.name: Mirzapur Handmade Durrie (GI)
Uttar Pradesh.description: Tightly woven, reversible flat-weave carpets favored for rugged durability and minimalist tribal designs.
